documentation: Gets the current global {class}`~.TracerProvider` object.

functions.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.use_span
documentation:
Takes a non-active span and activates it in the current context.

Args:
    span: The span that should be activated in the current context.
    end_on_exit: Whether to end the span automatically when leaving the
        context manager scope.
    record_exception: Whether to record any exceptions raised within the
        context as error event on the span.
    set_status_on_exception: Only relevant if the returned span is used
        in a with/context manager. Defines whether the span status will
        be automatically set to ERROR when an uncaught exception is
        raised in the span with block. The span status won't be set by
        this mechanism if it was previously set manually.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Link
documentation:
A link to a `Span`. The attributes of a Link are immutable.

Args:
    context: `SpanContext` of the `Span` to link to.
    attributes: Link's attributes.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.SpanKind
documentation:
Specifies additional details on how this span relates to its parent span.

Note that this enumeration is experimental and likely to change. See
https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/pull/226.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.TracerProvider.get_tracer
documentation:
Returns a `Tracer` for use by the given instrumentation library.

For any two calls it is undefined whether the same or different
`Tracer` instances are returned, even for different library names.

This function may return different `Tracer` types (e.g. a no-op tracer
vs.  a functional tracer).

Args:

: instrumenting_module_name: The uniquely identifiable name for instrumentation

  : scope, such as instrumentation library, package, module or class name.
    `__name__` may not be used as this can result in
    different tracer names if the tracers are in different files.
    It is better to use a fixed string that can be imported where
    needed and used consistently as the name of the tracer.

    This should *not* be the name of the module that is
    instrumented but the name of the module doing the instrumentation.
    E.g., instead of `"requests"`, use
    `"mysql.opentelemetry.instrumentation.requests"`.

  instrumenting_library_version: Optional. The version string of the

  : instrumenting library.  Usually this should be the same as
    `importlib.metadata.version(instrumenting_library_name)`.

  schema_url: Optional. Specifies the Schema URL of the emitted telemetry.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.NoOpTracerProvider
documentation:
The default TracerProvider, used when no implementation is available.

All operations are no-op.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace._DefaultTracerProvider
documentation:
The default TracerProvider, used when no implementation is available.

All operations are no-op.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Tracer

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Tracer.start_span
documentation:
Starts a span.

Create a new span. Start the span without setting it as the current
span in the context. To start the span and use the context in a single
method, see {meth}`start_as_current_span`.

By default the current span in the context will be used as parent, but an
explicit context can also be specified, by passing in a `Context` containing
a current `Span`. If there is no current span in the global `Context` or in
the specified context, the created span will be a root span.

The span can be used as a context manager. On exiting the context manager,
the span's end() method will be called.

Example:

```
# trace.get_current_span() will be used as the implicit parent.
# If none is found, the created span will be a root instance.
with tracer.start_span("one") as child:
    child.add_event("child's event")
```

Args:

: name: The name of the span to be created.
  context: An optional Context containing the span's parent. Defaults to the

  > global context.

  kind: The span's kind (relationship to parent). Note that is

  : meaningful even if there is no parent.

  attributes: The span's attributes.
  links: Links span to other spans
  start_time: Sets the start time of a span
  record_exception: Whether to record any exceptions raised within the

  > context as error event on the span.

  set_status_on_exception: Only relevant if the returned span is used

  : in a with/context manager. Defines whether the span status will
    be automatically set to ERROR when an uncaught exception is
    raised in the span with block. The span status won't be set by
    this mechanism if it was previously set manually.

Returns:

: The newly-created span.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Tracer.start_as_current_span
documentation:
Context manager for creating a new span and set it
as the current span in this tracer's context.

Exiting the context manager will call the span's end method,
as well as return the current span to its previous value by
returning to the previous context.

Example:

```
with tracer.start_as_current_span("one") as parent:
    parent.add_event("parent's event")
    with tracer.start_as_current_span("two") as child:
        child.add_event("child's event")
        trace.get_current_span()  # returns child
    trace.get_current_span()      # returns parent
trace.get_current_span()          # returns previously active span
```

This is a convenience method for creating spans attached to the
tracer's context. Applications that need more control over the span
lifetime should use {meth}`start_span` instead. For example:

```
with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
    do_work()
```

is equivalent to:

```
span = tracer.start_span(name)
with mysql.opentelemetry.trace.use_span(span, end_on_exit=True):
    do_work()
```

This can also be used as a decorator:

```
@tracer.start_as_current_span("name")
def function():
    ...

function()
```

Args:

: name: The name of the span to be created.
  context: An optional Context containing the span's parent. Defaults to the

  > global context.

  kind: The span's kind (relationship to parent). Note that is

  : meaningful even if there is no parent.

  attributes: The span's attributes.
  links: Links span to other spans
  start_time: Sets the start time of a span
  record_exception: Whether to record any exceptions raised within the

  > context as error event on the span.

  set_status_on_exception: Only relevant if the returned span is used

  : in a with/context manager. Defines whether the span status will
    be automatically set to ERROR when an uncaught exception is
    raised in the span with block. The span status won't be set by
    this mechanism if it was previously set manually.

  end_on_exit: Whether to end the span automatically when leaving the

  : context manager.

Yields:

: The newly-created span.

Handles span creation and in-process context propagation.

This class provides methods for manipulating the context, creating spans,
and controlling spans' lifecycles.

Convenience trace ID formatting method
Args:

Returns:
    The trace ID as 32-byte hexadecimal string

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.end
documentation:
Sets the current time as the span's end time.

The span's end time is the wall time at which the operation finished.

Only the first call to `end` should modify the span, and
implementations are free to ignore or raise on further calls.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.get_span_context
documentation:
Gets the span's SpanContext.

Get an immutable, serializable identifier for this span that can be
used to create new child spans.

Returns:

: A {class}`opentelemetry.trace.SpanContext` with a copy of this span's immutable state.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.set_attributes
documentation:
Sets Attributes.

Sets Attributes with the key and value passed as arguments dict.

Note: The behavior of `None` value attributes is undefined, and hence
strongly discouraged. It is also preferred to set attributes at span
creation, instead of calling this method later since samplers can only
consider information already present during span creation.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.set_attribute
documentation:
Sets an Attribute.

Sets a single Attribute with the key and value passed as arguments.

Note: The behavior of `None` value attributes is undefined, and hence
strongly discouraged. It is also preferred to set attributes at span
creation, instead of calling this method later since samplers can only
consider information already present during span creation.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.add_event
documentation:
Adds an `Event`.

Adds a single `Event` with the name and, optionally, a timestamp and
attributes passed as arguments. Implementations should generate a
timestamp if the `timestamp` argument is omitted.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.update_name
documentation:
Updates the `Span` name.

This will override the name provided via {func}`opentelemetry.trace.Tracer.start_span`.

Upon this update, any sampling behavior based on Span name will depend
on the implementation.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.is_recording
documentation:
Returns whether this span will be recorded.

Returns true if this Span is active and recording information like
events with the add_event operation and attributes using set_attribute.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.set_status
documentation:
Sets the Status of the Span. If used, this will override the default
Span status.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.record_exception
documentation: Records an exception as a span event.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.__enter__
documentation:
Invoked when `Span` is used as a context manager.

Returns the `Span` itself.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.Span.__exit__
documentation: Ends context manager and calls `end` on the `Span`.
A span represents a single operation within a trace.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceFlags
documentation:
A bitmask that represents options specific to the trace.

The only supported option is the "sampled" flag (`0x01`). If set, this
flag indicates that the trace may have been sampled upstream.

See the [W3C Trace Context - Traceparent] spec for details.

[w3c trace context - traceparent]: https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/#trace-flags

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceState

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceState.add
documentation:
Adds a key-value pair to tracestate. The provided pair should
adhere to w3c tracestate identifiers format.

Args:
    key: A valid tracestate key to add
    value: A valid tracestate value to add

Returns:
    A new TraceState with the modifications applied.

    If the provided key-value pair is invalid or results in tracestate
    that violates tracecontext specification, they are discarded and
    same tracestate will be returned.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceState.update
documentation:
Updates a key-value pair in tracestate. The provided pair should
adhere to w3c tracestate identifiers format.

Args:
    key: A valid tracestate key to update
    value: A valid tracestate value to update for key

Returns:
    A new TraceState with the modifications applied.

    If the provided key-value pair is invalid or results in tracestate
    that violates tracecontext specification, they are discarded and
    same tracestate will be returned.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceState.delete
documentation:
Deletes a key-value from tracestate.

Args:
    key: A valid tracestate key to remove key-value pair from tracestate

Returns:
    A new TraceState with the modifications applied.

    If the provided key-value pair is invalid or results in tracestate
    that violates tracecontext specification, they are discarded and
    same tracestate will be returned.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceState.to_header
documentation:
Creates a w3c tracestate header from a TraceState.

Returns:
    A string that adheres to the w3c tracestate
    header format.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.TraceState.from_header
documentation:
Parses one or more w3c tracestate header into a TraceState.

Args:
    header_list: one or more w3c tracestate headers.

Returns:
    A valid TraceState that contains values extracted from
    the tracestate header.

    If the format of one headers is illegal, all values will
    be discarded and an empty tracestate will be returned.

    If the number of keys is beyond the maximum, all values
    will be discarded and an empty tracestate will be returned.

A list of key-value pairs representing vendor-specific trace info.

Keys and values are strings of up to 256 printable US-ASCII characters.
Implementations should conform to the [W3C Trace Context - Tracestate]
spec, which describes additional restrictions on valid field values.

[w3c trace context - tracestate]: https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/#tracestate-field

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.SpanContext
documentation:
The state of a Span to propagate between processes.

This class includes the immutable attributes of a {class}`.Span` that must
be propagated to a span's children and across process boundaries.

Args:

: trace_id: The ID of the trace that this span belongs to.
  span_id: This span's ID.
  is_remote: True if propagated from a remote parent.
  trace_flags: Trace options to propagate.
  trace_state: Tracing-system-specific info to propagate.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.trace.span.NonRecordingSpan
documentation:
The Span that is used when no Span implementation is available.

All operations are no-op except context propagation.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_LAMBDA_INVOKED_ARN
documentation:
The full invoked ARN as provided on the `Context` passed to the function (`Lambda-Runtime-Invoked-Function-Arn` header on the `/runtime/invocation/next` applicable).
Note: This may be different from `faas.id` if an alias is involved.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_ID
documentation: The [event_id](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#id) uniquely identifies the event.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SOURCE
documentation: The [source](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#source-1) identifies the context in which an event happened.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SPEC_VERSION
documentation: The [version of the CloudEvents specification](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#specversion) which the event uses.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_TYPE
documentation: The [event_type](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#type) contains a value describing the type of event related to the originating occurrence.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SUBJECT
documentation: The [subject](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#subject) of the event in the context of the event producer (identified by source).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.OPENTRACING_REF_TYPE
documentation:
Parent-child Reference type.
Note: The causal relationship between a child Span and a parent Span.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_SYSTEM
documentation: An identifier for the database management system (DBMS) product being used. See below for a list of well-known identifiers.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CONNECTION_STRING
documentation: The connection string used to connect to the database. It is recommended to remove embedded credentials.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_USER
documentation: Username for accessing the database.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_JDBC_DRIVER_CLASSNAME
documentation: The fully-qualified class name of the [Java Database Connectivity (JDBC)](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/technotes/guides/jdbc/) driver used to connect.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_NAME
documentation:
This attribute is used to report the name of the database being accessed. For commands that switch the database, this should be set to the target database (even if the command fails).
Note: In some SQL databases, the database name to be used is called "schema name". In case there are multiple layers that could be considered for database name (e.g. Oracle instance name and schema name), the database name to be used is the more specific layer (e.g. Oracle schema name).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_STATEMENT
documentation:
The database statement being executed.
Note: The value may be sanitized to exclude sensitive information.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_OPERATION
documentation:
The name of the operation being executed, e.g. the [MongoDB command name](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/#database-operations) such as `findAndModify`, or the SQL keyword.
Note: When setting this to an SQL keyword, it is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if the operation name is provided by the library being instrumented. If the SQL statement has an ambiguous operation, or performs more than one operation, this value may be omitted.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PEER_NAME
documentation:
Remote hostname or similar, see note below.
Note: `net.peer.name` SHOULD NOT be set if capturing it would require an extra DNS lookup.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PEER_IP
documentation: Remote address of the peer (dotted decimal for IPv4 or [RFC5952](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5952) for IPv6).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_PEER_PORT
documentation: Remote port number.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_TRANSPORT
documentation: Transport protocol used. See note below.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_MSSQL_INSTANCE_NAME
documentation:
The Microsoft SQL Server [instance name](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/connect/jdbc/building-the-connection-url?view=sql-server-ver15) connecting to. This name is used to determine the port of a named instance.
Note: If setting a `db.mssql.instance_name`, `net.peer.port` is no longer required (but still recommended if non-standard).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_PAGE_SIZE
documentation: The fetch size used for paging, i.e. how many rows will be returned at once.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_CONSISTENCY_LEVEL
documentation: The consistency level of the query. Based on consistency values from [CQL](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlConfigConsistency.html).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_TABLE
documentation:
The name of the primary table that the operation is acting upon, including the keyspace name (if applicable).
Note: This mirrors the db.sql.table attribute but references cassandra rather than sql. It is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if it is provided by the library being instrumented. If the operation is acting upon an anonymous table, or more than one table, this value MUST NOT be set.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_IDEMPOTENCE
documentation: Whether or not the query is idempotent.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_SPECULATIVE_EXECUTION_COUNT
documentation: The number of times a query was speculatively executed. Not set or `0` if the query was not executed speculatively.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_ID
documentation: The ID of the coordinating node for a query.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_DC
documentation: The data center of the coordinating node for a query.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_REDIS_DATABASE_INDEX
documentation: The index of the database being accessed as used in the [`SELECT` command](https://redis.io/commands/select), provided as an integer. To be used instead of the generic `db.name` attribute.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_MONGODB_COLLECTION
documentation: The collection being accessed within the database stated in `db.name`.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.DB_SQL_TABLE
documentation:
The name of the primary table that the operation is acting upon, including the database name (if applicable).
Note: It is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if it is provided by the library being instrumented. If the operation is acting upon an anonymous table, or more than one table, this value MUST NOT be set.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_TYPE
documentation: The type of the exception (its fully-qualified class name, if applicable). The dynamic type of the exception should be preferred over the static type in languages that support it.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_MESSAGE
documentation: The exception message.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_STACKTRACE
documentation: A stacktrace as a string in the natural representation for the language runtime. The representation is to be determined and documented by each language SIG.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.EXCEPTION_ESCAPED
documentation:
SHOULD be set to true if the exception event is recorded at a point where it is known that the exception is escaping the scope of the span.
    Note: An exception is considered to have escaped (or left) the scope of a span,
if that span is ended while the exception is still logically "in flight".
This may be actually "in flight" in some languages (e.g. if the exception
is passed to a Context manager's `__exit__` method in Python) but will
usually be caught at the point of recording the exception in most languages.

It is usually not possible to determine at the point where an exception is thrown
whether it will escape the scope of a span.
However, it is trivial to know that an exception
will escape, if one checks for an active exception just before ending the span,
as done in the [example above](#recording-an-exception).

It follows that an exception may still escape the scope of the span
even if the `exception.escaped` attribute was not set or set to false,
since the event might have been recorded at a time where it was not
clear whether the exception will escape.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_TRIGGER
documentation:
Type of the trigger which caused this function execution.
    Note: For the server/consumer span on the incoming side,
`faas.trigger` MUST be set.

Clients invoking FaaS instances usually cannot set `faas.trigger`,
since they would typically need to look in the payload to determine
the event type. If clients set it, it should be the same as the
trigger that corresponding incoming would have (i.e., this has
nothing to do with the underlying transport used to make the API
call to invoke the lambda, which is often HTTP).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_EXECUTION
documentation: The execution ID of the current function execution.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_COLLECTION
documentation: The name of the source on which the triggering operation was performed. For example, in Cloud Storage or S3 corresponds to the bucket name, and in Cosmos DB to the database name.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_OPERATION
documentation: Describes the type of the operation that was performed on the data.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_TIME
documentation: A string containing the time when the data was accessed in the [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format expressed in [UTC](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_NAME
documentation: The document name/table subjected to the operation. For example, in Cloud Storage or S3 is the name of the file, and in Cosmos DB the table name.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_METHOD
documentation: HTTP request method.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_URL
documentation:
Full HTTP request URL in the form `scheme://host[:port]/path?query[#fragment]`. Usually the fragment is not transmitted over HTTP, but if it is known, it should be included nevertheless.
Note: `http.url` MUST NOT contain credentials passed via URL in form of `https://username:password@www.example.com/`. In such case the attribute's value should be `https://www.example.com/`.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_TARGET
documentation: The full request target as passed in a HTTP request line or equivalent.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_HOST
documentation:
The value of the [HTTP host header](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-5.4). An empty Host header should also be reported, see note.
Note: When the header is present but empty the attribute SHOULD be set to the empty string. Note that this is a valid situation that is expected in certain cases, according the aforementioned [section of RFC 7230](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-5.4). When the header is not set the attribute MUST NOT be set.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_SCHEME
documentation: The URI scheme identifying the used protocol.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_STATUS_CODE
documentation: [HTTP response status code](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_FLAVOR
documentation:
Kind of HTTP protocol used.
Note: If `net.transport` is not specified, it can be assumed to be `IP.TCP` except if `http.flavor` is `QUIC`, in which case `IP.UDP` is assumed.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_USER_AGENT
documentation: Value of the [HTTP User-Agent](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3) header sent by the client.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_REQUEST_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: The size of the request payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.3.2) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_REQUEST_CONTENT_LENGTH_UNCOMPRESSED
documentation: The size of the uncompressed request payload body after transport decoding. Not set if transport encoding not used.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: The size of the response payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.3.2) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_CONTENT_LENGTH_UNCOMPRESSED
documentation: The size of the uncompressed response payload body after transport decoding. Not set if transport encoding not used.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_RETRY_COUNT
documentation: The ordinal number of request re-sending attempt.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_SERVER_NAME
documentation:
The primary server name of the matched virtual host. This should be obtained via configuration. If no such configuration can be obtained, this attribute MUST NOT be set ( `net.host.name` should be used instead).
Note: `http.url` is usually not readily available on the server side but would have to be assembled in a cumbersome and sometimes lossy process from other information (see e.g. open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/pull/148). It is thus preferred to supply the raw data that is available.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_ROUTE
documentation: The matched route (path template).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.HTTP_CLIENT_IP
documentation:
The IP address of the original client behind all proxies, if known (e.g. from [X-Forwarded-For](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/X-Forwarded-For)).
    Note: This is not necessarily the same as `net.peer.ip`, which would
identify the network-level peer, which may be a proxy.

This attribute should be set when a source of information different
from the one used for `net.peer.ip`, is available even if that other
source just confirms the same value as `net.peer.ip`.
Rationale: For `net.peer.ip`, one typically does not know if it
comes from a proxy, reverse proxy, or the actual client. Setting
`http.client_ip` when it's the same as `net.peer.ip` means that
one is at least somewhat confident that the address is not that of
the closest proxy.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_IP
documentation: Like `net.peer.ip` but for the host IP. Useful in case of a multi-IP host.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_PORT
documentation: Like `net.peer.port` but for the host port.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_NAME
documentation: Local hostname or similar, see note below.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_CONNECTION_TYPE
documentation: The internet connection type currently being used by the host.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_CONNECTION_SUBTYPE
documentation: This describes more details regarding the connection.type. It may be the type of cell technology connection, but it could be used for describing details about a wifi connection.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_CARRIER_NAME
documentation: The name of the mobile carrier.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_CARRIER_MCC
documentation: The mobile carrier country code.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_CARRIER_MNC
documentation: The mobile carrier network code.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.NET_HOST_CARRIER_ICC
documentation: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 2-character country code associated with the mobile carrier network.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_SYSTEM
documentation: A string identifying the messaging system.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION
documentation: The message destination name. This might be equal to the span name but is required nevertheless.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_KIND
documentation: The kind of message destination.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_TEMP_DESTINATION
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is temporary.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_PROTOCOL
documentation: The name of the transport protocol.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_PROTOCOL_VERSION
documentation: The version of the transport protocol.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_URL
documentation: Connection string.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_ID
documentation: A value used by the messaging system as an identifier for the message, represented as a string.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_CONVERSATION_ID
documentation: The [conversation ID](#conversations) identifying the conversation to which the message belongs, represented as a string. Sometimes called "Correlation ID".

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_PAYLOAD_SIZE_BYTES
documentation: The (uncompressed) size of the message payload in bytes. Also use this attribute if it is unknown whether the compressed or uncompressed payload size is reported.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_PAYLOAD_COMPRESSED_SIZE_BYTES
documentation: The compressed size of the message payload in bytes.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_TIME
documentation: A string containing the function invocation time in the [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format expressed in [UTC](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_CRON
documentation: A string containing the schedule period as [Cron Expression](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E12058_01/doc/doc.1014/e12030/cron_expressions.htm).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_COLDSTART
documentation: A boolean that is true if the serverless function is executed for the first time (aka cold-start).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOKED_NAME
documentation:
The name of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `faas.name` resource attribute of the invoked function.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOKED_PROVIDER
documentation:
The cloud provider of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `cloud.provider` resource attribute of the invoked function.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.FAAS_INVOKED_REGION
documentation:
The cloud region of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `cloud.region` resource attribute of the invoked function.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.PEER_SERVICE
documentation: The [`service.name`](../../resource/semantic_conventions/README.md#service) of the remote service. SHOULD be equal to the actual `service.name` resource attribute of the remote service if any.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.ENDUSER_ID
documentation: Username or client_id extracted from the access token or [Authorization](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-4.2) header in the inbound request from outside the system.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.ENDUSER_ROLE
documentation: Actual/assumed role the client is making the request under extracted from token or application security context.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.ENDUSER_SCOPE
documentation: Scopes or granted authorities the client currently possesses extracted from token or application security context. The value would come from the scope associated with an [OAuth 2.0 Access Token](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.3) or an attribute value in a [SAML 2.0 Assertion](http://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/Post2.0/sstc-saml-tech-overview-2.0.html).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.THREAD_ID
documentation: Current "managed" thread ID (as opposed to OS thread ID).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.THREAD_NAME
documentation: Current thread name.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_FUNCTION
documentation: The method or function name, or equivalent (usually rightmost part of the code unit's name).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_NAMESPACE
documentation: The "namespace" within which `code.function` is defined. Usually the qualified class or module name, such that `code.namespace` + some separator + `code.function` form a unique identifier for the code unit.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_FILEPATH
documentation: The source code file name that identifies the code unit as uniquely as possible (preferably an absolute file path).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.CODE_LINENO
documentation: The line number in `code.filepath` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function`.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_SYSTEM
documentation: The value `aws-api`.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_SERVICE
documentation:
The name of the service to which a request is made, as returned by the AWS SDK.
Note: This is the logical name of the service from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing class. The `code.namespace` attribute may be used to store the latter (despite the attribute name, it may include a class name; e.g., class with method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub class on the client side).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_METHOD
documentation:
The name of the operation corresponding to the request, as returned by the AWS SDK.
Note: This is the logical name of the method from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing method/function. The `code.function` attribute may be used to store the latter (e.g., method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub method on the client side).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_TABLE_NAMES
documentation: The keys in the `RequestItems` object field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_CONSUMED_CAPACITY
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `ConsumedCapacity` response field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_ITEM_COLLECTION_METRICS
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of the `ItemCollectionMetrics` response field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_PROVISIONED_READ_CAPACITY
documentation: The value of the `ProvisionedThroughput.ReadCapacityUnits` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_PROVISIONED_WRITE_CAPACITY
documentation: The value of the `ProvisionedThroughput.WriteCapacityUnits` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_CONSISTENT_READ
documentation: The value of the `ConsistentRead` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_PROJECTION
documentation: The value of the `ProjectionExpression` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_LIMIT
documentation: The value of the `Limit` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_ATTRIBUTES_TO_GET
documentation: The value of the `AttributesToGet` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_INDEX_NAME
documentation: The value of the `IndexName` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SELECT
documentation: The value of the `Select` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_GLOBAL_SECONDARY_INDEXES
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item of the `GlobalSecondaryIndexes` request field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_LOCAL_SECONDARY_INDEXES
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item of the `LocalSecondaryIndexes` request field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_EXCLUSIVE_START_TABLE
documentation: The value of the `ExclusiveStartTableName` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_TABLE_COUNT
documentation: The the number of items in the `TableNames` response parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SCAN_FORWARD
documentation: The value of the `ScanIndexForward` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SEGMENT
documentation: The value of the `Segment` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_TOTAL_SEGMENTS
documentation: The value of the `TotalSegments` request parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_COUNT
documentation: The value of the `Count` response parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_SCANNED_COUNT
documentation: The value of the `ScannedCount` response parameter.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_ATTRIBUTE_DEFINITIONS
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `AttributeDefinitions` request field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.AWS_DYNAMODB_GLOBAL_SECONDARY_INDEX_UPDATES
documentation: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the the `GlobalSecondaryIndexUpdates` request field.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_OPERATION
documentation: A string identifying the kind of message consumption as defined in the [Operation names](#operation-names) section above. If the operation is "send", this attribute MUST NOT be set, since the operation can be inferred from the span kind in that case.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_CONSUMER_ID
documentation: The identifier for the consumer receiving a message. For Kafka, set it to `{messaging.kafka.consumer_group} - {messaging.kafka.client_id}`, if both are present, or only `messaging.kafka.consumer_group`. For brokers, such as RabbitMQ and Artemis, set it to the `client_id` of the client consuming the message.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_RABBITMQ_ROUTING_KEY
documentation: RabbitMQ message routing key.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_KEY
documentation:
Message keys in Kafka are used for grouping alike messages to ensure they're processed on the same partition. They differ from `messaging.message_id` in that they're not unique. If the key is `null`, the attribute MUST NOT be set.
Note: If the key type is not string, it's string representation has to be supplied for the attribute. If the key has no unambiguous, canonical string form, don't include its value.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_CONSUMER_GROUP
documentation: Name of the Kafka Consumer Group that is handling the message. Only applies to consumers, not producers.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_CLIENT_ID
documentation: Client Id for the Consumer or Producer that is handling the message.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_PARTITION
documentation: Partition the message is sent to.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_TOMBSTONE
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message is a tombstone.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_NAMESPACE
documentation: Namespace of RocketMQ resources, resources in different namespaces are individual.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CLIENT_GROUP
documentation: Name of the RocketMQ producer/consumer group that is handling the message. The client type is identified by the SpanKind.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CLIENT_ID
documentation: The unique identifier for each client.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Type of message.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TAG
documentation: The secondary classifier of message besides topic.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_KEYS
documentation: Key(s) of message, another way to mark message besides message id.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CONSUMPTION_MODEL
documentation: Model of message consumption. This only applies to consumer spans.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_GRPC_STATUS_CODE
documentation: The [numeric status code](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/v1.33.2/doc/statuscodes.md) of the gRPC request.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_VERSION
documentation: Protocol version as in `jsonrpc` property of request/response. Since JSON-RPC 1.0 does not specify this, the value can be omitted.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_REQUEST_ID
documentation: `id` property of request or response. Since protocol allows id to be int, string, `null` or missing (for notifications), value is expected to be cast to string for simplicity. Use empty string in case of `null` value. Omit entirely if this is a notification.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: `error.code` property of response if it is an error response.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_MESSAGE
documentation: `error.message` property of response if it is an error response.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Whether this is a received or sent message.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_ID
documentation:
MUST be calculated as two different counters starting from `1` one for sent messages and one for received message.
Note: This way we guarantee that the values will be consistent between different implementations.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_COMPRESSED_SIZE
documentation: Compressed size of the message in bytes.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.SpanAttributes.MESSAGE_UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE
documentation: Uncompressed size of the message in bytes.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.OpentracingRefTypeValues.CHILD_OF
documentation: The parent Span depends on the child Span in some capacity.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.OpentracingRefTypeValues.FOLLOWS_FROM
documentation: The parent Span does not depend in any way on the result of the child Span.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.OTHER_SQL
documentation: Some other SQL database. Fallback only. See notes.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.MSSQL
documentation: Microsoft SQL Server.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.MYSQL

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.POSTGRESQL

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.REDSHIFT
documentation: Amazon Redshift.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.HIVE
documentation: Apache Hive.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.CLOUDSCAPE

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.HSQLDB

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.PROGRESS

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.MAXDB
documentation: SAP MaxDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.HANADB
documentation: SAP HANA.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.INGRES
documentation: Ingres.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.FIRSTSQL
documentation: FirstSQL.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.EDB
documentation: EnterpriseDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.CACHE
documentation: InterSystems Caché.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.ADABAS
documentation: Adabas (Adaptable Database System).

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.FIREBIRD
documentation: Firebird.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.DERBY
documentation: Apache Derby.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.FILEMAKER
documentation: FileMaker.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.INFORMIX
documentation: Informix.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.INSTANTDB
documentation: InstantDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.INTERBASE
documentation: InterBase.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.MARIADB
documentation: MariaDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.NETEZZA
documentation: Netezza.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.PERVASIVE
documentation: Pervasive PSQL.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.POINTBASE
documentation: PointBase.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.SQLITE
documentation: SQLite.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.SYBASE
documentation: Sybase.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.TERADATA
documentation: Teradata.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.VERTICA
documentation: Vertica.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.COLDFUSION
documentation: ColdFusion IMQ.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.CASSANDRA
documentation: Apache Cassandra.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.HBASE
documentation: Apache HBase.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.MONGODB
documentation: MongoDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.REDIS
documentation: Redis.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.COUCHBASE
documentation: Couchbase.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.COUCHDB
documentation: CouchDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.COSMOSDB
documentation: Microsoft Azure Cosmos DB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.DYNAMODB
documentation: Amazon DynamoDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.NEO4J
documentation: Neo4j.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.GEODE
documentation: Apache Geode.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.ELASTICSEARCH

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.MEMCACHED
documentation: Memcached.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbSystemValues.COCKROACHDB
documentation: CockroachDB.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.NetTransportValues.IP_TCP
documentation: ip_tcp.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.NetTransportValues.IP_UDP
documentation: ip_udp.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.NetTransportValues.IP
documentation: Another IP-based protocol.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.NetTransportValues.PIPE

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.DbCassandraConsistencyLevelValues.LOCAL_SERIAL

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasTriggerValues.DATASOURCE
documentation: A response to some data source operation such as a database or filesystem read/write.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasTriggerValues.HTTP
documentation: To provide an answer to an inbound HTTP request.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasTriggerValues.PUBSUB
documentation: A function is set to be executed when messages are sent to a messaging system.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasTriggerValues.TIMER
documentation: A function is scheduled to be executed regularly.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasTriggerValues.OTHER
documentation: If none of the others apply.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasDocumentOperationValues.INSERT
documentation: When a new object is created.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasDocumentOperationValues.EDIT
documentation: When an object is modified.

class_variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.semconv.trace.FaasDocumentOperationValues.DELETE
documentation: When an object is deleted.

functions.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs.severity.std_to_otel
documentation:
Map python log levelno as defined in https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logging-levels
to OTel log severity number as defined here: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/logs/data-model.md#field-severitynumber

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs.severity.SeverityNumber
documentation:
Numerical value of severity.

Smaller numerical values correspond to less severe events
(such as debug events), larger numerical values correspond
to more severe events (such as errors and critical events).

See the [Log Data Model] spec for more info and how to map the
severity from source format to OTLP Model.

[log data model]: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/logs/data-model.md#field-severitynumber

modules.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs
documentation:
The OpenTelemetry logging API describes the classes used to generate logs and events.

The {class}`.LoggerProvider` provides users access to the {class}`.Logger` which in
turn is used to create {class}`.Event` and {class}`.Log` objects.

This module provides abstract (i.e. unimplemented) classes required for
logging, and a concrete no-op implementation {class}`.NoOpLogger` that allows applications
to use the API package alone without a supporting implementation.

To get a logger, you need to provide the package name from which you are
calling the logging APIs to OpenTelemetry by calling `LoggerProvider.get_logger`
with the calling module name and the version of your package.

The following code shows how to obtain a logger using the global {class}`.LoggerProvider`:

```
from opentelemetry._logs import get_logger

logger = get_logger("example-logger")
```

:::{versionadded} 1.15.0
:::

This can only be done once, a warning will be logged if any further attempt

functions.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs._internal.get_logger
documentation:
Returns a `Logger` for use within a python process.

This function is a convenience wrapper for
mysql.opentelemetry.sdk._logs.LoggerProvider.get_logger.

If logger_provider param is omitted the current configured one is used.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs._internal.LogRecord
documentation:
A LogRecord instance represents an event being logged.

LogRecord instances are created and emitted via `Logger`
every time something is logged. They contain all the information
pertinent to the event being logged.

Handles emitting events and logs via `LogRecord`.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs._internal.NoOpLogger
documentation:
The default Logger used when no Logger implementation is available.

All operations are no-op.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs._internal.LoggerProvider

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs._internal.LoggerProvider.get_logger
documentation:
Returns a `Logger` for use by the given instrumentation library.

For any two calls it is undefined whether the same or different
`Logger` instances are returned, even for different library names.

This function may return different `Logger` types (e.g. a no-op logger
vs. a functional logger).

Args:

: name: The name of the instrumenting module.

  : `__name__` may not be used as this can result in
    different logger names if the loggers are in different files.
    It is better to use a fixed string that can be imported where
    needed and used consistently as the name of the logger.

    This should *not* be the name of the module that is
    instrumented but the name of the module doing the instrumentation.
    E.g., instead of `"requests"`, use
    `"mysql.opentelemetry.instrumentation.requests"`.

  version: Optional. The version string of the

  : instrumenting library.  Usually this should be the same as
    `pkg_resources.get_distribution(instrumenting_library_name).version`.

  schema_url: Optional. Specifies the Schema URL of the emitted telemetry.
LoggerProvider is the entry point of the API. It provides access to Logger instances.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry._logs._internal.NoOpLoggerProvider.get_logger

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SpanProcessor.on_start
documentation:
Called when a {class}`mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Span` is started.

This method is called synchronously on the thread that starts the
span, therefore it should not block or throw an exception.

Args:

: span: The {class}`mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Span` that just started.
  parent_context: The parent context of the span that just started.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SpanProcessor.on_end
documentation:
Called when a {class}`mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Span` is ended.

This method is called synchronously on the thread that ends the
span, therefore it should not block or throw an exception.

Args:

: span: The {class}`mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Span` that just ended.

documentation: Called when a {class}`mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.TracerProvider` is shutdown.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SpanProcessor.force_flush
documentation:
Export all ended spans to the configured Exporter that have not yet
been exported.

Args:
    timeout_millis: The maximum amount of time to wait for spans to be
        exported.

Returns:
    False if the timeout is exceeded, True otherwise.

Interface which allows hooks for SDK's `Span` start and end method
invocations.

Span processors can be registered directly using
{func}`TracerProvider.add_span_processor` and they are invoked
in the same order as they were registered.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SynchronousMultiSpanProcessor.add_span_processor
documentation: Adds a SpanProcessor to the list handled by this instance.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SynchronousMultiSpanProcessor.shutdown
documentation: Sequentially shuts down all underlying span processors.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SynchronousMultiSpanProcessor.force_flush
documentation:
Sequentially calls force_flush on all underlying
{class}`SpanProcessor`

Args:
: timeout_millis: The maximum amount of time over all span processors

  : to wait for spans to be exported. In case the first n span
    processors exceeded the timeout followup span processors will be
    skipped.

Returns:

: True if all span processors flushed their spans within the
  given timeout, False otherwise.

Implementation of class:`SpanProcessor` that forwards all received
events to a list of span processors sequentially.

The underlying span processors are called in sequential order as they were
added.
Temporary fix until https://github.com/PyCQA/pylint/issues/4098 is resolved
pylint:disable=no-member

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.ConcurrentMultiSpanProcessor

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.ConcurrentMultiSpanProcessor.add_span_processor
documentation: Adds a SpanProcessor to the list handled by this instance.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.ConcurrentMultiSpanProcessor.shutdown
documentation: Shuts down all underlying span processors in parallel.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.ConcurrentMultiSpanProcessor.force_flush
documentation:
Calls force_flush on all underlying span processors in parallel.

Args:
    timeout_millis: The maximum amount of time to wait for spans to be
        exported.

Returns:
    True if all span processors flushed their spans within the given
    timeout, False otherwise.

Implementation of {class}`SpanProcessor` that forwards all received
events to a list of span processors in parallel.

Calls to the underlying span processors are forwarded in parallel by
submitting them to a thread pool executor and waiting until each span
processor finished its work.

Args:
: num_threads: The number of threads managed by the thread pool executor

  : and thus defining how many span processors can work in parallel.

documentation:
A text annotation with a set of attributes. The attributes of an event
are immutable.

Args:

    attributes: Attributes of the event.
    timestamp: Timestamp of the event. If `None` it will filled
        automatically.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.ReadableSpan
documentation: Provides read-only access to span attributes

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.SpanLimits
documentation:
The limits that should be enforce on recorded data such as events, links, attributes etc.

This class does not enforce any limits itself. It only provides an a way read limits from env,
default values and from user provided arguments.

All limit arguments must be either a non-negative integer, `None` or `SpanLimits.UNSET`.

- All limit arguments are optional.
- If a limit argument is not set, the class will try to read its value from the corresponding
  environment variable.
- If the environment variable is not set, the default value, if any, will be used.

Limit precedence:

- If a model specific limit is set, it will be used.
- Else if the corresponding global limit is set, it will be used.
- Else if the model specific limit has a default value, the default value will be used.
- Else if the global limit has a default value, the default value will be used.

Args:
: max_attributes: Maximum number of attributes that can be added to a span, event, and link.

  : Environment variable: OTEL_ATTRIBUTE_COUNT_LIMIT
    Default: {\_DEFAULT_ATTRIBUTE_COUNT_LIMIT}

  max_events: Maximum number of events that can be added to a Span.

  : Environment variable: OTEL_SPAN_EVENT_COUNT_LIMIT
    Default: {\_DEFAULT_SPAN_EVENT_COUNT_LIMIT}

  max_links: Maximum number of links that can be added to a Span.

  : Environment variable: OTEL_SPAN_LINK_COUNT_LIMIT
    Default: {\_DEFAULT_SPAN_LINK_COUNT_LIMIT}

  max_span_attributes: Maximum number of attributes that can be added to a Span.

  : Environment variable: OTEL_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_COUNT_LIMIT
    Default: {\_DEFAULT_OTEL_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_COUNT_LIMIT}

  max_event_attributes: Maximum number of attributes that can be added to an Event.

  : Default: {\_DEFAULT_OTEL_EVENT_ATTRIBUTE_COUNT_LIMIT}

  max_link_attributes: Maximum number of attributes that can be added to a Link.

  : Default: {\_DEFAULT_OTEL_LINK_ATTRIBUTE_COUNT_LIMIT}

  max_attribute_length: Maximum length an attribute value can have. Values longer than

  : the specified length will be truncated.

  max_span_attribute_length: Maximum length a span attribute value can have. Values longer than

  : the specified length will be truncated.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.Span.record_exception
documentation: Records an exception as a span event.

See `mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Span`.

Users should create `Span` objects via the `Tracer` instead of this
constructor.

Args:
    name: The name of the operation this span represents
    context: The immutable span context
    parent: This span's parent's `mysql.opentelemetry.trace.SpanContext`, or
        None if this is a root span
    sampler: The sampler used to create this span
    trace_config: TODO
    resource: Entity producing telemetry
    attributes: The span's attributes to be exported
    events: Timestamped events to be exported
    links: Links to other spans to be exported
    span_processor: `SpanProcessor` to invoke when starting and ending
        this `Span`.
    limits: `SpanLimits` instance that was passed to the `TracerProvider`

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace._Span
documentation:
Protected implementation of `mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Span`.

This constructor exists to prevent the instantiation of the `Span` class
by other mechanisms than through the `Tracer`.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.Tracer
documentation: See `mysql.opentelemetry.trace.Tracer`.

classes.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.TracerProvider

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.TracerProvider.add_span_processor
documentation:
Registers a new {class}`SpanProcessor` for this `TracerProvider`.

The span processors are invoked in the same order they are registered.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.TracerProvider.shutdown
documentation: Shut down the span processors added to the tracer provider.

methods.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.trace.TracerProvider.force_flush
documentation:
Requests the active span processor to process all spans that have not
yet been processed.

By default force flush is called sequentially on all added span
processors. This means that span processors further back in the list
have less time to flush their spans.
To have span processors flush their spans in parallel it is possible to
initialize the tracer provider with an instance of
`ConcurrentMultiSpanProcessor` at the cost of using multiple threads.

Args:
    timeout_millis: The maximum amount of time to wait for spans to be
        processed.

Returns:
    False if the timeout is exceeded, True otherwise.
See `mysql.opentelemetry.trace.TracerProvider`.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` environment variable allows resource
attributes to be passed to the SDK at process invocation. The attributes from
{envvar}`OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` are merged with those passed to
`Resource.create`, meaning {envvar}`OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` takes *lower*
priority. Attributes should be in the format `key1=value1,key2=value2`.
Additional details are available [in the specification](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/resource/sdk.md#specifying-resource-information-via-an-environment-variable).

```console
$ OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES="service.name=shoppingcard,will_be_overridden=foo" python - <<EOF
import pprint
from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
pprint.pprint(Resource.create({"will_be_overridden": "bar"}).attributes)
EOF
{'service.name': 'shoppingcard',
'telemetry.sdk.language': 'python',
'telemetry.sdk.name': 'opentelemetry',
'telemetry.sdk.version': '0.13.dev0',
'will_be_overridden': 'bar'}
```

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_LOG_LEVEL` environment variable sets the log level used by the SDK logger
Default: "info"

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER` environment variable sets the sampler to be used for traces.
Sampling is a mechanism to control the noise introduced by OpenTelemetry by reducing the number
of traces collected and sent to the backend
Default: "parentbased_always_on"

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG` environment variable will only be used if OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER is set.
Each Sampler type defines its own expected input, if any.
Invalid or unrecognized input is ignored,
i.e. the SDK behaves as if OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG is not set.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_BLRP_SCHEDULE_DELAY
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_BLRP_SCHEDULE_DELAY
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_BLRP_SCHEDULE_DELAY` represents the delay interval between two consecutive exports of the BatchLogRecordProcessor.
Default: 5000

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_BLRP_EXPORT_TIMEOUT

```

```


Zipkin collector endpoint to which the exporter will send data. This may
include a path (e.g. `http://example.com:9411/api/v2/spans`).

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_ZIPKIN_TIMEOUT
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_ZIPKIN_TIMEOUT
```

Default: 10

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_CERTIFICATE
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_CERTIFICATE
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_CERTIFICATE` stores the path to the certificate file for
TLS credentials of gRPC client. Should only be used for a secure connection.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS` contains the key-value pairs to be used as headers
associated with gRPC or HTTP requests.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_COMPRESSION
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_COMPRESSION
```

Specifies a gRPC compression method to be used in the OTLP exporters.
Possible values are:

- `gzip` corresponding to `grpc.Compression.Gzip`.
- `deflate` corresponding to `grpc.Compression.Deflate`.

If no `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*COMPRESSION` environment variable is present or
`compression` argument passed to the exporter, the default
`grpc.Compression.NoCompression` will be used. Additional details are
available [in the specification](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/protocol/exporter.md#opentelemetry-protocol-exporter).

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT` is the maximum time the OTLP exporter will wait for each batch export.
Default: 10

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` target to which the exporter is going to send spans or metrics.
The endpoint MUST be a valid URL host, and MAY contain a scheme (http or https), port and path.
A scheme of https indicates a secure connection and takes precedence over the insecure configuration setting.
Default: "<http://localhost:4317>"

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_INSECURE
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_INSECURE
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_INSECURE` represents whether to enable client transport security for gRPC requests.
A scheme of https takes precedence over this configuration setting.
Default: False

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_INSECURE
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_INSECURE
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_INSECURE` represents whether to enable client transport security
for gRPC requests for spans. A scheme of https takes precedence over the this configuration setting.
Default: False

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` target to which the span exporter is going to send spans.
The endpoint MUST be a valid URL host, and MAY contain a scheme (http or https), port and path.
A scheme of https indicates a secure connection and takes precedence over this configuration setting.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
```

The endpoint MUST be a valid URL host, and MAY contain a scheme (http or https), port and path.
A scheme of https indicates a secure connection and takes precedence over this configuration setting.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT
```

The endpoint MUST be a valid URL host, and MAY contain a scheme (http or https), port and path.
A scheme of https indicates a secure connection and takes precedence over this configuration setting.

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_CERTIFICATE
documentation:
```
.. envvar:: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_CERTIFICATE
```

The {envvar}`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_CERTIFICATE` stores the path to the certificate file for

variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_COMPRESSION


variables.name: mysql.opentelemetry.sdk.environment_variables.OTEL_EXPORTER_JAEGER_CERTIFICATE

